source: Synthesised from BMW Service Training (Aftersales) documents: 'E65 Audio System', 'Central Body Electronics', '7 Series Bus Charts E65', 'E65 Rear Entertainment', and 'E65/E66 Model Redesign Participant's Manual' (October 2004).
notes: Reference sheet on how each E65 subsystem is wired and which bus it uses. Use this to know what your ELM327 can reach vs what needs ISTA + K-DCAN cable.

topics.title: Engine codes by model year
topics.summary: Your 2007 730Li is post-'Model Redesign' (MU), built after November 2004 facelift. Engine is N52.
details: E65MU 730i / 730Li (post-MU, 11/2004+) — N52 engine, 190 kW / 258 bhp / 300 Nm  <-- YOUR CAR
E65MU 740i / 740Li (post-MU) — N62TU, 225 kW / 306 bhp / 390 Nm
E65MU 750i / 750Li (post-MU) — N62TU, 270 kW / 367 bhp
GCC builds: typically 730Li and 750Li dominate. Long-wheelbase (Li) suffix common.

topics.title: Audio system architecture
topics.summary: E65 audio is built around the ASK (Audio System Controller) acting as MOST bus network master. Two variants: Basic Hi-Fi (180W, 10 speakers) or LOGIC 7 (420W, 13 speakers, SA 677).
details: ASK = Audio System Controller — central audio router AND MOST bus network master. Handles all audio signals + warning gongs + PDC beeps.
AVT = Antenna Tuner with Diversity. Located in REAR LEFT C-pillar (not in head unit). Connects via MOST. No coaxial cable to head unit.
Basic Hi-Fi amp — 180W total, 40W/channel, drives sub-woofers only (mid/tweeter run direct from ASK). NO DIAGNOSTIC CAPABILITY — invisible to ISTA. Location: LEFT REAR corner of trunk.

LOGIC 7 amp (SA 677) — 420W total, 40W mid / 70W woofer. Full MOST connection. Full diagnostic capability via ISTA. Location: LEFT REAR corner of trunk. 7-band EQ + surround sound + frequency-dependent delay per speaker.
Speakers Basic Hi-Fi (10 total): 100mm mid + 44mm tweeter each front door, 2x 210mm sub under driver+passenger seats, 2x 100mm mid + 2x 44mm tweeter in rear shelf.
Speakers LOGIC 7 (13 total): adds center channel mid in dashboard + mid-range in rear doors.
CD changer: 6-disc Alpine unit, passenger-side dashboard, behind decorative strip. MOST-connected.
All audio (radio, CD, nav voice, telephone, gongs, PDC) is digital 44.1 kHz on MOST bus.

topics.title: Bus topology — what your ELM327 can reach
topics.summary: E65 uses 5+ separate buses. Your ELM327 only speaks D-CAN. Everything else needs a K+DCAN cable or BMW ICOM.
details: D-CAN (Diagnostic CAN, 500 kbps) — OBD pins 6/14. Reaches: DME (N52 engine), DSC (stability/ABS), EGS (ZF 6HP transmission), Airbag. YOUR APP REACHES THESE.
K-CAN P (Comfort Periphery) — door modules (SMFA seat, FH window, ZV lock), TMFAT door switches. NOT reachable from OBD with ELM327.
K-CAN S (Comfort System) — CAS (central access), JBE (junction box), light control, KOMBI cluster, IHKA HVAC. NOT reachable.
PT-CAN (Powertrain CAN) — sensor pre-processing on engine. Bridged to D-CAN via DME — most useful signals appear on D-CAN.
MOST (fiber optic, 25 Mbps) — audio + video ring. CCC head unit, Logic 7 amp, CD changer, antenna tuner, rear entertainment, TV tuner. NOT reachable from OBD.
byteflight (later: FlexRay) — airbag satellites + crash sensors only. Not for diagnostics.
Gateway: ZGM (Zentral-Gateway-Modul) bridges these buses for inter-module messages BUT does NOT forward UDS diagnostic requests between buses on E65 — that's F-series only.
Practical: with ELM327 you can read engine, transmission, brakes, airbag. You CANNOT read audio, comfort, lights, seats, body modules.

topics.title: Module abbreviation glossary
topics.summary: BMW uses German abbreviations everywhere. Here's what they mean.
details: ASK — Audio System Controller (MOST master, audio router)
AVT — Antenna Tuner (rear left C-pillar)
CAS — Car Access System (immobilizer, central locking master, key recognition)
CCC — Car Communication Computer (iDrive head unit with navigation)
DME — Digital Motor Electronics (engine ECU — your N52 ECU)
DSC — Dynamic Stability Control (ABS + stability + traction)
EGS — Electronic Transmission Control (transmission ECU — ZF 6HP19Z for 730Li)
EKP — Electric Fuel Pump control
ELV — Electronic Steering Lock
FBD — Radio Remote-Control Receiver
FH — Window Lift Control
FRM — Footwell Module (lights, indicators, K-CAN gateway)
FZV — Remote Central Locking
GA6HP19Z — your ZF 6-speed gearbox (730Li specific)
GS19 — synonym for the 6HP19 trans control unit
GWS — Gear selector electronics (shifter)
HKL — Powered Trunk Lid
IBS — Intelligent Battery Sensor (battery state-of-charge, on negative terminal)
IHKA — Integrated Heating / Air Conditioning (climate control / HVAC)
JBE — Junction Box Electronics (fuse + relay + body gateway)
KOMBI — instrument cluster
PDC — Park Distance Control (parking sensors)
RDC — Tire Pressure Monitoring
SBSL — Side Indicator Light
SCA — Soft Close Automatic (door close motor)
SHD — Sunroof / Slide-Tilt Roof
SMBF — Passenger Seat Module (K-CAN)
SMFA — Driver Seat Module (K-CAN)
SZL — Steering Column Switch Cluster
TMFAT — Driver Door Module
TMBFT — Passenger Door Module
TOEHK — Trunk Lid Outside Pushbutton
TOEHKI — Trunk Lid Inside Pushbutton (in cabin)
ULF — Universal Cordless Phone Interface (Bluetooth/handset)
ZGM — Central Gateway Module (bridges D-CAN / K-CAN / MOST)
ZKE — Central Body Electronics
ZV — Central Locking

topics.title: Central locking system
topics.summary: Locking control is in CAS (Car Access System); output ops distributed across multiple door modules over K-CAN P/S.
details: CAS = master. Doors / boot / fuel flap controlled separately by their own modules over K-CAN.

Operating points: driver door lock cylinder, FBD remote, cabin lock button, trunk-inside button (TOEHKI), trunk-outside button (TOEHK), trunk-lid button (TOEHKK if HKL fitted), hotel switch in centre console.
Trunk lock cylinder is mechanical-emergency-only — no electrical signal.
Functions: Lock, Double-lock, Selective Unlock (driver only), Full Unlock, Soft-Close (optional).
Soft-close motors (SCA): one per door + trunk. Powered cinching to fully closed position.
Crash unlock and 'key-inserted' forced double-unlock are handled by CAS.
Anti-theft alarm communicates via K-CAN S.
Common faults: comfort access antenna failure in door handle, ELV (steering lock) module death, soft-close motor failure (lifecycle ~150k cycles).

topics.title: Rear entertainment system (SA option)
topics.summary: Optional rear-seat entertainment with two 6.5" screens, rear DVD changer, rear armrest control, cordless headsets. All MOST-connected.
details: 6.5" displays mounted behind front headrests (one driver-side, one passenger-side).
Video Module 5 Drive — TV tuner / video source switching.
DVD changer (separate from front audio CD changer).
Rear armrest control center (BZMF — Rear Centre Control Unit).
FCON — Rear Compartment Controller (rear compartment master).
SG FD — Rear Display Control Unit.
Cordless headset interface (IR or RF) so rear passengers don't disturb front.
All on MOST bus. NOT reachable via ELM327 — only ISTA via K-DCAN.
Common failure: cordless headset RF receiver, headphone hum from ground loop.

topics.title: Power windows architecture
topics.summary: Decentralised: each door has its own module (Driver Door Module, Passenger Door Module). K-CAN P communication.
details: Each door module runs its own window motor and reads its own switch panel.
Driver door switches send command to passenger doors via K-CAN P to operate other windows.
Anti-pinch: motor current monitored — if obstacle detected, window reverses ~10cm.
Comfort closing (with remote): hold lock button → all windows close + sunroof closes + mirrors fold.
If a window doesn't work from main switch but works from local switch = K-CAN P comm fault. Door loom break in hinge boot is the usual cause.

topics.title: GCC-spec differences (UAE / Saudi / Qatar / Kuwait / Bahrain / Oman)
topics.summary: Your 2007 730Li was built for Gulf Cooperation Council markets. Mechanically identical to Euro spec, but climate-tuned and equipment-loaded.
details: ENGINE: Same N52B30 as Euro spec — no GCC-specific tuning. BUT GCC fuel was 95 RON minimum at the time; modern UAE Special 98 is fine.
TRANSMISSION: Same ZF GA6HP19Z — no GCC variant.
COOLING UPRATED: Larger radiator core, second auxiliary fan, larger A/C condenser, additional A/C expansion valve. Tuned for 50°C+ ambient.
A/C: Dual evaporator on Li models, rear A/C standard. Compressor cycles harder than Euro — first failure point in GCC.
BATTERY: AGM 90Ah standard (same as Euro), but 5-7 year life in GCC heat vs 8-10 in Europe.
PAINT/INTERIOR: Heat-reflective paint codes more common. Beige/cream interior dominant for sun reflection.
GLASS: Often factory privacy glass (rear), heat-reflective windscreen.
TYRES: 245/55R17 standard, 245/50R18 common. GCC heat = check pressure WARM (35 psi cold becomes 40 psi after highway).
EQUIPMENT BIAS: Comfort Access, Top Hi-Fi (Logic 7 SA 677), rear sun blinds, ventilated front seats, soft-close doors — almost always fitted.
OMITTED IN GCC: Heated steering wheel, heated washer jets, ski hatch (rare). Diesel variants not sold.
EMISSIONS: Euro 3/4 tune (lighter than late Euro 5/6). SAI (secondary air injection) present but operates less than cold climates.
SERVICE INTERVALS: Shorter than Euro. Oil at 8k km (not 10k). Brake fluid at 18 months (not 24). A/C service every 18 months.
RUST: Not really an issue in GCC dry climate — major advantage. Suspension and brake parts age slower than UK/EU cars.
WHAT YOU MIGHT SEE THAT EURO CARS DON'T: A/C compressor failure earlier, battery shorter life, rubber seals stiffer, but body intact.

topics.title: What to do when your app cannot reach a module
topics.summary: ELM327 limitations and the K-DCAN workaround.
details: If your app shows NO DATA for a fault you suspect, the module is probably on K-CAN or MOST.
Symptoms reachable via ELM327 (D-CAN): engine running issues, misfires, transmission fault, ABS/DSC fault, airbag warning.
Symptoms NOT reachable: audio dead, windows broken, lights, seats, sunroof, HVAC fault codes (live HVAC sensor data IS partly available via DME).
K-DCAN cable (~€30 eBay) gives you K-CAN access. Combined with INPA or ISTA-D software (Windows) you can read everything.
BMW ICOM Next adapter (~€300+) gives full MOST + K-CAN + D-CAN + FlexRay access, official ISTA support.
For a 2007 730Li, a K-DCAN cable + ISTA + Rheingold is usually enough for everything except MOST programming.
Your phone app's job: dashboard, fault codes from DME/DSC/EGS, live data, sensor browser. For body/comfort: K-DCAN + laptop.
